message:
Fix WS logout, MCP validation, refresh button, dynamic card fallback (#4917)

- #4906: Close all WebSocket connections for a user on logout via
  Hub.DisconnectUser(), wired into AuthHandler.Logout. Previously,
  established WS sessions remained active after token revocation.

- #4907: Add "not_found" error classification for non-existent cluster
  names in k8s.classifyError(). handleK8sError now returns 404 with a
  consistent error body instead of 500 for unknown clusters.

- #4908: Standardize MCP error response format across all failure types.
  All error responses now include clusterStatus, errorType, and
  errorMessage fields. Connectivity errors return 503 (was 200),
  not-found returns 404, and unknown errors return 500 — all with the
  same JSON structure.

- #4909: triggerRefresh in useRefreshIndicator now calls
  triggerAllRefetches() in addition to the primary refetchFn. This
  ensures clicking the dashboard refresh button reloads all card data
  hooks (useCachedPods, useCachedDeployments, etc.), not just cluster
  data.

- #4910: Harden DynamicCard against invalid/missing config. Guard
  against undefined config, missing dynamicCardId, malformed
  cardDefinition, and missing apiEndpoint for API-sourced Tier1 cards.
  Validation checks are placed after hooks to respect Rules of Hooks.

Fixes #4906, #4907, #4908, #4909, #4910

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 import { useState, useCallback, useRef } from 'react'
+import { triggerAllRefetches } from '../lib/modeTransition'
 
 // Minimum time to show the refresh indicator on user-triggered refreshes.
 // Kept short for snappy feedback without causing visible flicker.
@@ -22,8 +23,13 @@ export function useRefreshIndicator(refetchFn: () => void, _resetKey?: string) {
       clearTimeout(timerRef.current)
     }
 
+    // Call the primary refetch function (e.g., cluster data)
     refetchFn()
 
+    // Also trigger all registered cache refetches so individual card data
+    // hooks (useCachedPods, useCachedDeployments, etc.) reload their data (#4909).
+    triggerAllRefetches()
+
     timerRef.current = setTimeout(() => {
       setShowIndicator(false)
       timerRef.current = null
